arts.ttl: Fabrizio Romano transfer news: Cunha BOMB, GAME OVER for Man Utd, Nkunku SURPRISE
arts.sum: Romano confirmed the interest of Arsenal, Nottingham Forest, Chelsea and Manchester United in Cunha for the winter transfer window, and says these clubs have a chance to sign him again in the summer. Matheus Cunha may have signed a new contract with Wolverhampton Wanderers, but that doesn’t mean he will remain with the Molineux club beyond the summer, with Romano announcing that the Brazil international has a release clause that makes him a viable target.

arts.ttl: Fabrizio Romano says Arsenal didn’t go for Matheus Cunha because of who they could sign in summer

arts.ttl: Injured Wolves player sets date for return – Edging towards training
arts.sum: Wolverhampton Wanderers’ Enso González is close to joining the rest of the group for training, according to La Nación. The 19-year-old arrived at Wolves from Paraguayan side Club Libertad in August 2023 and waited until the end of last season to make his senior team debut. arts.ttl: “Then you get this in your face” – Player says Blackburn Rovers have put him in ‘impossible position’
arts.sum: On Friday, Blackburn Rovers manager John Eustace confirmed Arnór Sigurdsson has been left out of their 25-man Championship squad. The Iceland international hasn’t started this season and last featured for Blackburn Rovers against Watford on October 26th. arts.sum: Matheus Cunha's form for Wolves has caught the attention of Arsenal, Aston Villa, Chelsea and Tottenham.

arts.ttl: Cunha release clause revealed ahead of summer interest in Wolves forward
arts.sum: Matheus Cunha’s new contract with Wolves contains a release clause of £62.5m that can be activated this summer. Cunha was linked with several Premier League rivals during the January transfer window, but recommitted his future to Wolves with a new deal.
